calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Continued To Dec-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appeared by zoom for Shannon Tague (Executor).

Ms. Hinshaw stated that she has the receipt for distribution of the reserve, that the receipt came from Central America, that it came as a photograph, and that she submitted it three times for filing and it has been rejected. Ms. Hinshaw also stated that she resubmitted the receipt yesterday and it is for an amount of $3,600.00. Status on distribution of the reserve is continued to December 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Nov-17-2020 Continued To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw for Shannon Tague.

The Court raised the issue of the ongoing $100.00 a month obligation which technically creates a testamentary trust. Ms. Hinshaw stated that the payments are to continue for three years from the Decedent's date of death or when the Beneficiary graduates from school, that her Client is willing to stay on and that her Client really cares about getting the money to the Beneficiary. The Court stated that this is highly unusual, but that it is prepared to allow this. The Court granted the First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. (subject to review of the order) with a reserve amount to cover it. Status on distribution of the reserve is set for November 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Filing Of Bond.
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Petitiner Shannon Tague, in pro per; Bruce Reading.

The Court noted that probate procedures were complex, and inquired if Ms. Tague felt able to proceed without representation by counsel. She responded in the affirmative. The Court asked the estimated value of the estate. Ms. Tague the total value of the estate was $1,600,000, the main asset being the home valued at $1,100,000, and with a remaining mortgage of $450,000. The Court stated that since there were other beneficiaries of the estate and no legal counsel, he would exercise his power to require the posting of a bond. The Court granted the petition for probate of will (subject to review of the order). Bond was set at $1,100,000. A status hearing re posting of bond was set for January 29, 2018 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Wan. (Not reported)
